title: Cohen-Macaulayness of Local Models via Shellability of the Admissible Set
speaker: Xuhua He
abstract:
Simonyi 101
The singularities of integral models of Shimura varieties are encoded in their local models, schemes over the 
p
-adic integers whose special fibers are unions of affine Schubert cells. A fundamental question is whether these local models are Cohen-Macaulay.In this talk, I will present a solution for local models with arbitrary parahoric level structure, valid uniformly across all residue characteristics. The proof is centered on a combinatorial property of the admissible set, which parametrizes the cells in the special fiber.  We prove that the admissible set is dual EL-shellable, thereby resolving a conjecture of Görtz from over two decades ago. From this purely combinatorial result, we deduce the Cohen-Macaulay property for the corresponding local models.This work provides a uniform, characteristic-independent approach that contrasts with and complements prior geometric methods. I will explain the key combinatorial ideas and their translation into this geometric consequence.

proof_stages.label: Local models as the singularity problem
proof_stages.summary: The talk reduces singularities of integral models of Shimura varieties to local models, whose special fibers should be understood as unions of affine Schubert varieties rather than only through explicit moduli equations.

proof_stages.label: Admissible-set and parahoric indexing
proof_stages.summary: The affine Weyl group, Bruhat order, admissible set Adm(mu), and its parahoric variant are introduced as the indexing posets for the affine-Schubert stratification of the special fiber, with semi-normalization included in small characteristic.

proof_stages.label: Shellability criterion for Cohen-Macaulayness
proof_stages.summary: Görtz's conjectural dual EL-shellability of the admissible set is explained as the combinatorial condition that lets one attach Schubert components successively in codimension one, thereby giving a Cohen-Macaulay special fiber and hence a Cohen-Macaulay local model.

proof_stages.label: Global dual EL-shellability theorem
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem states that for arbitrary coweight and parahoric level, after adjoining needed endpoints, the augmented admissible set is dual EL-shellable with an explicit shelling order.

proof_stages.label: Explicit edge labeling
proof_stages.summary: The proof labels ordinary cover relations by their affine reflections or roots, labels augmented top edges by chosen finite Weyl group representatives of conjugates of mu, and orders these labels using reflection orders and Bruhat order.

proof_stages.label: Uniform verification of the EL conditions
proof_stages.summary: Recent combinatorics of the quantum Bruhat graph, quantum paths, acute cones, and length positivity are used to verify the unique increasing-chain and lexicographic-minimality conditions uniformly, including the difficult parahoric cases.

proof_stages.label: Cohen-Macaulay consequences and extensions
proof_stages.summary: Combining the shellability theorem with the local-model characterization gives Cohen-Macaulayness for broad classes of local and integral models, yields Cohen-Macaulay partial unions of components, and motivates analogous questions for EKOR and basic-locus strata.

examples.name: Hyperspecial level as a single Schubert component
examples.note: In response to a question, the speaker recalls that hyperspecial local models were already known to be Cohen-Macaulay because the special fiber is irreducible, namely one affine Schubert variety.
examples.engagement_reason: the hyperspecial case is used to verify Cohen-Macaulayness by reducing the special fiber to one irreducible affine Schubert component whose Cohen-Macaulayness is known
